steps.title: Set up
steps.content: Rearrange $x^2 + 13x = -9$ to standard form by adding 9 to both sides: $$x^2 + 13x + 9 = 0$$ So $a = 1$, $b = 13$, $c = 9$.

steps.title: Solve
steps.content: Compute the discriminant: $b^2 - 4ac = 169 - 36 = 133$. Substitute into the quadratic formula: $$x = \frac{-13 \pm \sqrt{133}}{2}$$
